title: Bomb threats prompt lockdown, evacuations at several Northeast Ohio school districts
text: Five Northeast Ohio districts received bomb threats on the morning of May 7. Alliance City Schools evacuated both Alliance High School and Rockhill Elementary after a robocall; police found nothing. Cleveland Metropolitan School District said threats to East Tech High School were swatting incidents and buildings were deemed safe.

records.evidence_quote: threats ... were swatting incidents and buildings were deemed safe

title: Liverpool school calls off classes after bomb threat; police treat it as a hoax
text: A hoax bomb-threat email was sent to several Merseyside schools. LIPA Primary and High School called off classes; Chesterfield High School in Crosby was placed in temporary lockdown before resuming. Merseyside Police confirmed no school was required to close and treated the matter as a hoax.

records.evidence_quote: treated the matter as a hoax
placed in temporary lockdown before resuming

title: Swatting incidents becoming more common for school districts, officials say
text: Swatting incidents are becoming more common for school districts, law-enforcement officials say, as hoax calls rise nationally. The article discusses the broader trend and its toll but does not report a single specific incident at a named school.

label.reason: General trend/analysis coverage — no single datable incident at a named facility.

title: Some New Jersey school districts dismiss early because of heat wave
text: Several New Jersey districts dismissed students early on Tuesday due to high temperatures during a heat wave. No safety threat was involved.

label.reason: Non-safety disruption (weather); no hostile threat and no safety protective action.
